proof: [§10] For he has made plain to us through all the Prophets that he needs neither sacrifices nor burnt-offerings nor oblations, saying in one place, What is the multitude of your sacrifices unto me? saith the Lord. I am full of burnt offerings and desire not the fat of lambs and the blood of bulls and goats, not even when ye come to appear before me. For who has required these things at your hands? Henceforth shall ye tread my court no more.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly states that the traditional system of material sacrifices (animals, fat, blood) has been rejected by God for the author's community. This represents a removal of a previously standard religious practice in favor of a 'new law'.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the entire burgeoning Christian community and its relationship to Jewish ritual tradition.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to observe Sabbaths and New Moons
proof: [§10] Incense is an abomination to me. I cannot away with your new moons and sabbaths. These things then he abolished in order that the new law of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is without the yoke of necessity, might have its oblation not made by man.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author identifies specific calendar-based religious rights (Sabbaths and New Moons) as being 'abolished' for those following the 'new law', marking a clear loss of these traditional communal resources.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule defines the religious boundaries for the entire group addressed by the author.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to cancellation of unjust contracts
proof: [§21] But to us he says, Behold this is the fast which I chose, saith the Lord, loose every bond of wickedness, set loose the fastenings of harsh agreements, send away the bruised in forgiveness, and tear up every unjust contract
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text outlines the 'fast' expected of the community, which includes the concrete legal/economic action of tearing up unjust contracts and loosening 'harsh agreements'. This grants a resource (debt relief/forgiveness) to the disadvantaged group.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule specifically targets those in debt or under harsh contracts within the reach of the community's influence.

proof: [§21] give to the hungry thy bread, and if thou seest a naked man clothe him, bring the homeless into thy house, and if thou seest a humble man, despise him not
extracted_rules.reasoning: The community is commanded to provide material resources—bread, clothing, and housing—to the poor and homeless. This constitutes a customary right to communal support for these groups.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Poverty and homelessness were widespread conditions in the ancient world, affecting a significant portion of the population.

proof: [§28] It is ours: but in this way did they finally lose it when Moses had just received it... And Moses understood and cast the two tables out of his hands, and their covenant was broken, in order that the covenant of Jesus the Beloved should be sealed in our hearts in hope of his faith.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author argues that the 'covenant'—which in this context functions as the primary legal and foundational status of the people of God—was permanently lost by the Jews due to idolatry and is now exclusive to the author's group.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to an entire ethnic/religious group (the Jews) within the author's historical narrative.

proof: [§28] Do not by retiring apart live alone as if you were already made righteous, but come together and seek out the common good.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author issues a communal rule against physical or social withdrawal (living alone/retiring apart), requiring members to stay integrated within the assembly. This is a restriction on individual movement and association for the group's members.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This is a general behavioral mandate for all members of the addressed community.

proof: [§35] It is ours: but in this way did they finally lose k when Moses had just received it, for the Scripture says: And Moses was in the mount fasting forty days and forty nights, and he received the covenant from the Lord, tables of stone written with the finger of the hand of the Lord. [§36] But they turned to idols and lost it. For thus saith the Lord: Moses, Moses, go down quickly, for thy people, whom thou broughtest forth out of the land of Egypt, have broken the Law. And Moses understood and cast the two tables out of his hands, and their covenant was broken
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author argues that the Jewish people (Israel) permanently lost their legal/religious claim to the covenant due to the golden calf incident, transferring the status of 'covenant people' exclusively to the Christian community.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to an entire ethnic/religious group within the Roman and Mediterranean world of the author's time.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to pursue the common good
proof: [§38] Do not by retiring apart live alone as if you were already made righteous, but come together and seek out the common good.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text establishes a rule of assembly and collective pursuit of resources ('common good') rather than individual or private living for members of the group.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule governs the social and economic behavior of all members of the specified religious community.

proof: [§40] The Lord will judge the world without respect of persons. Each will receive according to his deeds.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a rule of universal legal accountability where status or identity (respect of persons) is disregarded in favor of individual actions.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule is framed as applying to every human being in the world.

proof: [§58] We then are they whom he brought into the good land. [...] Thus therefore we also, being nourished on the faith of the promise and by the word, shall live and possess the earth. [...] when we ourselves also have been made perfect as heirs of the covenant of the Lord.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author identifies his group as the 'new people' who are the true heirs of the land/earth, a concrete resource originally promised to Israel but now legally reassigned in the author's worldview.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to all individuals identified with the specified 'new people'.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to rule beasts and birds
proof: [§58] For it is concerning us that the scripture says that he says to the Son, Let us make man after our image and likeness, and let them rule the beasts of the earth, and the birds of heaven, and the fishes of the sea. [...] See then, we have been created afresh, as he says again in another Prophet, [...] To rule implies authority, so that one may give commandments and have domination.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author claims that the 'newly created' people are the ones granted the concrete authority to dominate and utilize the animal resources of the earth, though he notes this rule is perfected in the future.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the group defined by their religious status as the 'new creation'.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to eat sacrificial meat during communal fasts
proof: [§81] The commandment was written, Whosoever does not keep the fast shall die the death [...]. [§82] [4] What then does he say in the Prophet? And let them eat of the goat which is offered in the fast for all their sins. Attend carefully,—and let all the priests alone eat the entrails unwashed with vinegar. [§83] [5] Why? [...] therefore you alone shall eat, while the people fast and mourn in sackcloth and ashes.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a specific ritual rule where priests are exempted from the general fast to consume certain parts of the sacrifice (the goat's entrails), while the rest of the 'people' are strictly required to fast under penalty of death.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule applies specifically to the priestly class within the context of a specific religious fast day.

extracted_rules.resource: Protection from capital punishment for dietary choices
proof: [§81] The commandment was written, Whosoever does not keep the fast shall die the death [...]. [§83] [5] therefore you alone shall eat, while the people fast and mourn in sackcloth and ashes.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text cites a commandment stating that anyone (referring to the general populace/non-priests) who does not observe the fast faces the penalty of death.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the broad majority of the population who are not priests.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to perform public purification rituals
proof: [§90] boys then take the ashes and put them into vessels and bind scarlet wool on sticks [...] and that the boys all sprinkle the people thus one by one in order that they all be purified from their sins? [...] [§93] [3] The boys who sprinkle are they who preached to us the forgiveness of sins, and the purification of the heart, to whom he gave the power of the Gospel to preach
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author describes a customary or legalistic ritual (identified with Israel) where young boys, specifically, are entrusted with the concrete power to sprinkle the community for purification, a role the author then maps onto apostolic authority.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: The rule limits a specific ritual action to a specific age group (boys).

extracted_rules.resource: Validity of physical circumcision as a religious seal
proof: [§98] But moreover the circumcision in which they trusted has been abolished. For he declared that circumcision was not of the flesh, but they erred because an evil angel was misleading them.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author explicitly states that the physical circumcision practiced by the Jewish people is no longer a valid or recognized legal/divine seal for their covenant, claiming it was a result of error rather than legitimate divine law.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This affects an entire ethnic/religious group relative to their standing in the perceived covenant.

extracted_rules.resource: Power to preach and offer forgiveness
proof: [§93] [3] The boys who sprinkle are they who preached to us the forgiveness of sins, and the purification of the heart, to whom he gave the power of the Gospel to preach, and there are twelve as a testimony to the tribes
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text identifies a specific small group (the twelve) as having been granted the 'power' to preach and mediate purification, distinguishing them from the general audience of the message.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies only to the specific college of twelve individuals.

extracted_rules.resource: Access to baptism for remission of sins
proof: [§122] Concerning the water it has been written with regard to Israel that they will not receive the baptism that brings the remission of sins, but will build for themselves.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author explicitly states that the group (Israel) is barred from or fails to receive the specific civic/religious mechanism of baptism which grants the 'remission of sins' (a form of legal/spiritual pardon), contrasting them with the author's own group.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule applies to a large ethnic/religious collective (Israel) within the context of the author's theological-legal framework.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to participate in judgment or counsel
proof: [§128] Therefore the wicked shall not rise up in judgment, nor sinners in the counsel of the righteous, for the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous, and the way of the ungodly shall perish.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a formal exclusion from 'judgment' and 'counsel', which are concrete civic/communal functions. While 'wicked' is a moral term, in this context it refers to a specific social stratum identified by the author as those outside the covenantal legal structure.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: It targets a broad section of the population defined by their lack of adherence to specific community laws.

proof: [§113] Thou shalt not, he means, consort with or become like such men who are utterly ungodly and who are already condemned to death
[§114] Because thou shalt not, he means, become a corruptor of the young, or become like such men
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author interprets dietary laws as social rules against specific behaviors (pedastry/corruption of young). He notes that men who engage in these acts are 'already condemned to death', indicating a severe legal/customary loss of bodily integrity/right to life.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: The rule targets a specific sub-group of men accused of specific social crimes.

extracted_rules.resource: Freedom to consort with diverse social groups
proof: [§111] He mentioned the swine for this reason: you shall not consort, he means, with men who are like swine
[§113] Thou shalt not, he means, consort with or become like such men who are utterly ungodly
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text outlines a series of prohibitions against associating (consorting) with various groups (those who plunder, the ungodly, adulterers). This represents a customary restriction on the freedom of movement and social interaction for members of the author's community.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: These rules govern the daily social interactions of all members within the 'righteous' community regarding the rest of society.

proof: [§116] nor shalt thou cleave to women who are unclean and who commit lawlessness in their mouths.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly forbids 'cleaving' (associating/marrying) to a specific category of women described as 'unclean' due to 'lawlessness in their mouths'. This indicates a social/legal exclusion and loss of standing for women based on perceived impurity or behavioral patterns.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: It applies specifically to women categorized as 'unclean' or 'lawless' under specific communal standards.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to ritual purification from sin
proof: [§132] He means to say that we go down into the water full of sins and foulness, and we come up bearing the fruit of fear in our hearts, and having hope on Jesus in the Spirit.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a formal communal ritual (baptism) where the group 'we' (Christians) gains the specific resource of being cleansed from legal/moral 'foulness' through water, which is a concrete religious right in this early Christian context.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the entire burgeoning Christian community but is distinct from the general population.

proof: [§139] Moreover, though Moses commanded them:—You shall have neither graven nor molten image for your God, yet he makes one himself to show a type of Jesus.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text cites a specific legal command given to the people of Israel forbidding the creation of physical images for worship, which is a restriction on their religious expression.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule applied to the entire nation of Israel according to the referenced Law.

proof: [§152] Behold I have made thee, Abraham, the father of the Gentiles who believe in God in uncircumcision.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author argues that the 'inheritance' or 'covenant' (a bundle of legal and spiritual rights) is granted to Gentiles based on belief rather than the traditional legal requirement of circumcision.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule identifies a very broad category (all non-Jews who believe).

extracted_rules.resource: Priority in paternal blessing and inheritance
proof: [§150] And Jacob crossed his hands, and placed his right hand on the head of Ephraim, the second and younger son, and blessed him; and Joseph said to Jacob, Change thy right hand on to the head of Manasses, for he is my first-born son. And Jacob said to Joseph, I know it, my child, I know it; but the greater shall serve the less, and this one shall indeed be blessed.
extracted_rules.reasoning: While the custom normally grants the elder son the primary blessing, the text explicitly describes a rule-reversal where the 'greater' (elder) is made to 'serve the less' (younger), stripping the elder of his traditional birthright.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This refers to the specific legal status of the 'first-born' within family structures.

proof: [§154] He has given it. But they were not worthy to receive it because of their sins.
[§156] And Moses perceived that they had made themselves again molten images, and he cast them out of his hands, and the tables of the covenant of the Lord were broken.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author asserts that the 'former people' lost their concrete legal standing (the covenant) because of a specific breach of law (idolatry), leading to the physical destruction of the legal instrument (the tables).
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the entire Jewish nation in the author's historical-theological framework.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to celebrate the eighth day
proof: [§163] Wherefore we also celebrate with gladness the eighth day in which Jesus also rose from the dead, and was made manifest, and ascended into Heaven.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author distinguishes the Christian practice of celebrating the 'eighth day' (Sunday) from the traditional Jewish Sabbath, which he claims is no longer acceptable to God. This establishes a new customary rule for the group of believers.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the entire community of early Christians/believers addressed by the letter.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to a physical temple facility
proof: [§173] Again, it was made manifest that the city and the temple and the people of Israel were to be delivered up. For the Scripture says, And it shall come to pass in the last days that the Lord shall deliver the sheep of his pasture, and the sheep-fold, and their tower to destruction.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author argues that the physical temple of the Jews was intentionally delivered to destruction by God because of their 'error' in hoping in a building, effectively stripping the group of their central religious site.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This affects the entire Jewish population/nation as described by the author.

proof: [§181] When we received the remission of sins, and put our hope on the Name, we became new, being created again from the beginning; wherefore God truly dwells in us, in the habitation which we are.
[§183] This is a spiritual temple being built for the Lord.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author defines a new rule for the believers where the individual's body/heart replaces the physical temple as the site of divine presence and ritual purity.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to the specific collective of people who have received 'remission of sins' as defined by the text.

proof: [§160] I have given thee for a covenant of the people, for a light to the Gentiles, to open the eyes of the blind, and to bring forth from their fetters those that are bound.
[§161] Lo, I have made thee a light for the Gentiles, to be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly states that the covenant and salvation, previously restricted or unrevealed, are now granted to Gentiles.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule concerns an extremely large category of people (all non-Jews).

proof: [§195] Thou shalt not procure abortion, thou shalt not commit infanticide.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly forbids abortion and infanticide as part of the 'Way of Light,' establishing a communal rule where infants and the unborn are granted the right to life and protection from lethal harm.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule applies to all children/infants within the community and prohibits actions by any member.

proof: [§195] Thou shalt not withhold thy hand from thy son or from thy daughter, but shalt teach them the fear of God from their youth up.
extracted_rules.reasoning: Parents are commanded not to neglect their children's upbringing but to actively provide them with religious knowledge, establishing a child's right to receive this specific cultural/religious resource.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: The rule is specific to the relationship between parents and their children within the community.

proof: [§197] thou shalt not command in bitterness thy slave or handmaid who hope on the same God, lest they cease to fear the God who is over you both
extracted_rules.reasoning: The rule limits the manner in which a master can exercise authority, specifically prohibiting harsh or 'bitter' treatment of enslaved persons who share the same faith.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: The rule specifically concerns the sub-group of enslaved persons who share the master's religion.

proof: [§197] Thou shalt obey thy masters as a type of God in modesty and fear
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text reinforces the social hierarchy by commanding slaves to obey their masters as they would a deity, codifying their lack of personal autonomy.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to all enslaved individuals within the household structure described.

proof: [§198] Thou shalt share all things with thy neighbour and shall not say that they are thy own property
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text mandates a radical sharing of material goods, effectively granting neighbors (often the needy in this context) a claim on what would otherwise be private property.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This is a general rule for all members of the community regarding their possessions.

proof: [§205] who attend not to the cause of the widow and orphan... without pity for the poor, working not for him who is oppressed with toil
extracted_rules.reasoning: By condemning those who ignore the 'cause' of widows and orphans, the text establishes a customary rule that these vulnerable groups are entitled to legal attention and social support.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: It targets specific vulnerable categories (widows, orphans, the oppressed).

proof: [§208] I beseech those who are in high positions, if you will receive any counsel of my goodwill, have among yourselves those to whom you may do good; fail not.
extracted_rules.reasoning: Those in 'high positions' (wealthy/powerful) are specifically commanded to use their resources for the benefit of others, creating a normative drain on their private wealth for communal benefit.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Directly addresses those in senior or wealthy social tiers.
